items.headline: Marvell stock soars ~32% as NVIDIA's Jensen Huang says it could be the next trillion-dollar company
items.whyItMatters: An unusually direct endorsement from the dominant AI-systems vendor (and a Marvell shareholder) reframes the bull case and the multiple. Bankers should watch for the durability of the re-rating and any read-through to AVGO and the broader custom-silicon/optical complex.

items.headline: NVIDIA invests $2B in Marvell and announces strategic NVLink Fusion / silicon-photonics / AI-RAN partnership
items.whyItMatters: A ~2.5% strategic stake plus deep platform integration embeds Marvell's custom XPUs and scale-up networking inside NVIDIA's semi-custom AI-factory ecosystem. Validates Marvell's roadmap, but also ties its fortunes more closely to NVIDIA and raises questions on margin/economics of NVLink-linked designs.

items.headline: Marvell stock surges 18% as CEO Matt Murphy points to continuing AI demand: 'Do you see me blinking?'
items.whyItMatters: Strong FY2026 results and an upbeat FY2027 trajectory eased fears of a custom-silicon slowdown. The sharp positive reaction signals how sensitive the name is to AI-demand narrative and management confidence.

items.headline: Marvell reports record Q4 FY2026 revenue of $2.219B (+22% YoY) and FY2026 revenue of $8.195B (+42%)
items.whyItMatters: Confirms AI data center as the growth engine (~74% of FY2026 revenue) with non-GAAP EPS up 81% YoY. Sets the baseline for FY2027 guidance acceleration that underpins the bull thesis.

items.headline: J.P. Morgan reiterates Overweight, raises Marvell price target to $135 from $130 after Q4 print
items.whyItMatters: Sell-side reaffirmation post-earnings supports the data-center/XPU growth narrative; useful as a sentiment marker on the custom-silicon ramp.

items.headline: Marvell posts record Q2 FY2026 revenue of $2.01B (+58% YoY); data center $1.49B (+69%)
items.whyItMatters: Demonstrated the steep AI-driven ramp but also surfaced gross-margin pressure (non-GAAP ~59.4%, down YoY) and rising competition from Broadcom and Taiwanese ASIC houses - a key debate for the stock.

items.headline: Infineon completes $2.5B acquisition of Marvell's Brightlane Automotive Ethernet business
items.whyItMatters: Portfolio pruning that exits a non-core automotive franchise (~$225-250M FY2026 revenue) to sharpen focus on AI data center and free up capital. A clean read on management's capital-allocation discipline.

items.headline: Marvell hosts Custom AI Silicon investor webinar; raises custom/data-center TAM and reiterates multi-year targets
items.whyItMatters: Management quantified the custom-silicon opportunity (>$10B custom revenue target by FY2029, 50+ opportunities across 10+ customers), framing the long-term TAM that supports the AI bull case.

items.headline: Marvell reports record Q1 FY2026 revenue of ~$1.895B (+63% YoY) on strong AI demand
items.whyItMatters: Marked the inflection where custom-silicon programs entered volume production and data center scaled past two-thirds of revenue - the start of the FY2026 AI ramp that drove the stock all year.

items.headline: Marvell agrees to sell Automotive Ethernet business to Infineon for $2.5B in all-cash deal
items.whyItMatters: Signaled a strategic narrowing to AI/data-center connectivity and compute, monetizing an asset at a premium; sets up the August 2025 close and a cleaner growth profile.
